title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Custom  Role Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Custom  Role Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to Identity and Access Management (IAM) role creation, deletion and updating activities.
input_row.rationale: Google Cloud IAM provides predefined roles that give granular access to specific Google Cloud Platform resources and prevent unwanted access to other resources. However, to cater to organization-specific needs, Cloud IAM also provides the ability to create custom roles. Project owners and administrators with the Organization Role Administrator role or the IAM Role Administrator role can create custom roles. Monitoring role creation, deletion and updating activities will help in identifying any over- privileged role at early stages.
input_row.audit: From Console: Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric is present with filter text: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of zero(0) for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName = "google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true.
input_row.remediation: From Console: Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName = "google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") 1. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 2. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the advanced logs query. 3. Click Create Metric. Create a prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the new metric that was just created under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page displays. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value ensures that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 1. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 2. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed Alert Policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit control is sufficiently detailed and specifies the use of GCP APIs, Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), and GCP CLI commands to verify metrics and policies. This indicates that the control can be fully automated since it can be queried without the need for manual intervention.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_metric_alert_custom_role_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would perform the following checks:
  1. Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to retrieve all log metrics.
     - **Check**: Ensure that there is at least one log metric with the following filter: 
       ```
       resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole")
       ```
     - **Pass Criteria**: Metric must exist and match the specified filter.

  2. Retrieve alerting policies by executing `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`.
     - **Check**: Inspect the output for at least one alert policy that includes:
       - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter`: This should be set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<metric_name>"` where `<metric_name>` corresponds to the identified log metric.
       - `enabled` should be set to `true`.
     - **Pass Criteria**: At least one enabled alert policy with the correct conditions is present.

  3. Additionally, ensure that the output for alert policies contains the appropriate notification channels configured to trigger notifications as specified in the control.
     - **Check**: Validate that the metrics and alert policy settings are appropriate for the organization’s needs based on the summary provided in the benchmark control.
     - **Pass Criteria**: Notification channels must be set up correctly.

  4. Compute any impacts such as additional charges for increased logging usage as noted in the control's impact section.

By implementing this automation program, you will ensure that compliance with the specified CIS control is regularly checked and maintained effectively, without the need for manual reviews.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The tasks outlined involve querying existing configurations and settings, which are well-suited for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` is correct for retrieving log metrics. However, the filter syntax in the description should be verified against the actual logs to ensure it captures the intended events.
- The command `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` is valid, but note that the use of `alpha` commands may indicate that the feature is not fully stable or generally available. It would be prudent to check if a stable version is available.
- The program name `gcp_logging_metric_alert_custom_role_changes` is descriptive and follows a reasonable naming convention, though it could be slightly more specific to include "iam" to reflect the focus on IAM role changes.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- Consider verifying the filter syntax against actual log entries to ensure it captures all relevant IAM role changes. The filter should be tested in a live environment to confirm its effectiveness.
- Ensure that the `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` command is the most stable and recommended version for production use. If a stable version exists, it should be used instead.
- The program name could be improved to `gcp_logging_iam_role_change_alerts` to more clearly indicate the focus on IAM role changes.
- It would be beneficial to include a step to verify the configuration of notification channels more explicitly, ensuring they are set up to deliver alerts to the appropriate recipients.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and aligns with GCP capabilities. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is appropriate for automation.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_logging_iam_role_change_alerts` for clarity. Verify the filter syntax against actual logs and confirm the use of stable `gcloud` commands for production environments. Ensure explicit verification of notification channel configurations.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the control can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The tasks involve querying existing configurations and settings, which are well-suited for automation. The necessary data for compliance verification is accessible via GCP APIs, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_metric_alert_iam_role_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: IAM Roles, Logging Metrics
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to retrieve log metrics.
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format json` to retrieve alerting policies.
- Properties to Check:
  - Log Metric Filter: `resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole")`
  - Alert Policy: `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` should match the log metric and be enabled.
- Pass Criteria: 
  - A log metric exists with the specified filter.
  - An alert policy is present and enabled for the log metric.
- Fail Criteria: 
  - Absence of the log metric with the specified filter.
  - No enabled alert policy for the log metric.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation, and the minor critique regarding the use of `alpha` commands was noted. The stable version of the command should be used if available.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
